sops.title: Alcoholic Beverages and Tastings SOP
important_rules: Reporting to work under the influence or consuming alcohol during work is strictly prohibited.
Alcohol consumption is only permitted for approved tastings/trainings.
Tastings are sample portions not exceeding 2 oz per pour.
Tastings must be scheduled and conducted privately away from guests.
Only authorized personnel may be behind the bar during business hours.
Staff may not patronize bar/lounge before/during/after shift without prior approval.

sections.title: Procedure
sections.body_html:
• 1. Use alcohol only under approved tasting/training exceptions.
  • 2. For tastings, limit pours to 2 oz samples.
  • 3. Conduct tastings only for approved staff training or product evaluation.
  • 4. Schedule tastings in advance with vendor or management.
  • 5. Hold tastings in private area away from guests.
  • 6. Allow only bar staff, venue managers, and F&B executives behind bar during business hours.
  • 7. Require culinary staff to coordinate with venue management for alcohol transfers used in food prep.
  • 8. Require Engineering/IT/Accounting staff to be accompanied by venue management when accessing alcoholic beverages.

sops.title: IRD Delivery and Pick-Up Track Sheet Procedure
important_rules: Every delivery/pick-up must be logged.
Use complete required columns for accountability.

sections.title: Procedure
sections.body_html:
• 1. Open IRD Delivery and Pick Up Number tracking sheet for date.
  • 2. Record date and team member name.
  • 3. Record room number and guest name for each delivery.
  • 4. Record time of delivery.
  • 5. Record time guest called for pick-up when applicable.
  • 6. Record pickup team member name.
  • 7. Record actual time of pickup.
  • 8. Add notes for exceptions/issues.

sops.title: IRD Pick-Up Sheet SOP
important_rules: Process is mandatory for all team members handling IRD deliveries and tray removals.
Guest floors must be checked every 30–45 minutes.
No tray should remain outside a guest room longer than 30 minutes unattended.
Guest call-in pick-up must be completed within 15–20 minutes.
Missing items must be noted and reported before end of shift.

sections.title: Procedure
sections.body_html:
• 1. Maintain the IRD Pick-Up Sheet daily at the designated IRD station or host stand.
  • 2. For each delivery record: date, room number, guest name, time delivered, call time (if applicable), pick-up time, server name, and notes.
  • 3. At delivery, record room number, delivery time, and server name.
  • 4. Inform guest about tray removal timing (call IRD or pickup within ~60–90 minutes).
  • 5. Check guest floors every 30–45 minutes and remove hallway trays immediately.
  • 6. Record actual tray pick-up time on the sheet.
  • 7. When a guest calls for pickup, record call time and assign team member immediately.
  • 8. Complete pickup within 15–20 minutes and update sheet.
  • 9. During pickup verify/return trays, tray jacks, silverware, coffee pots, and condiment sets.
  • 10. Report missing equipment/items to management before shift end.

sops.title: Pool Bar Tip Pooling and Tip-Out SOP
important_rules: Tips are pooled per shift (lunch and dinner are separate pools).
Tip-out deductions occur before pool distribution.
Barback tip-out is 10% total or 5% per shift for doubles.
All servers must verify sales/tip-outs before closing.
Discrepancies must be reported immediately.

sections.title: Procedure
sections.body_html:
• 1. Operate lunch shift pool (10:00 AM–4:30 PM) and dinner shift pool (4:30 PM–11:00 PM) independently.
  • 2. Calculate each employee share by hours worked in that shift.
  • 3. Deduct required tip-outs from each shift total (10% to barback; doubles 5% + 5%).
  • 4. Calculate tip-outs from each server's individual sales and combine into pool.
  • 5. After tip-outs, total remaining tips for shift pool.
  • 6. Add total hours of participating employees.
  • 7. Distribute tips proportionally by hours worked.
  • 8. Have servers verify sales and tip-out totals before close.
  • 9. MOD/management oversees final distribution.
  • 10. Report and resolve discrepancies immediately.

sops.title: Restaurant Allergy SOP
important_rules: Never guess allergen information; confirm with chef.
Allergy tickets must be clearly marked as ALLERGY ALERT.
Server must verbally notify kitchen and manager.
Call 911 immediately for allergic reaction symptoms.

sections.title: Procedure
sections.body_html:
• 1. Ask each table about food allergies.
  • 2. Document allergies clearly in POS.
  • 3. Verbally confirm allergy details with guest before placing order.
  • 4. If unsure about ingredients, confirm with chef before advising guest.
  • 5. Flag allergy tickets in POS and print/mark clearly as ALLERGY ALERT.
  • 6. Server personally informs chef/kitchen staff of allergy.
  • 7. Notify supervisor/manager on duty about allergy order.
  • 8. Server delivers dish directly and confirms allergen omission with guest.
  • 9. If reaction symptoms occur, call 911 immediately.
  • 10. Notify manager on duty and stay with guest until EMS arrives.
  • 11. If available, assist with guest EpiPen only if requested or guest cannot self-administer.

sops.title: Tula OpenTable Phone Reservation SOP
important_rules: Search by phone first to avoid duplicate profiles.
Collect and confirm all reservation details before ending call.

sections.title: Procedure
sections.body_html:
• 1. Answer the call professionally.
  • 2. Collect guest name, phone number, party size, date/time preference, and special requests/allergies/celebrations.
  • 3. Open OpenTable Reservations tab.
  • 4. Select date and party size; review available times.
  • 5. Start reservation flow and choose shift/service type (breakfast/lunch/dinner).
  • 6. Select time slot.
  • 7. Input reservation details: guest name, phone, notes.
  • 8. Create or locate guest profile by searching phone first.
  • 9. If guest not found, create new profile with full guest information and phone number.
  • 10. Add email if guest wants confirmation.
  • 11. Capture add-ons/specs: occasion, dietary needs, seating preference, highchair/accessibility, setup requests.
  • 12. Select/enter staff name.
  • 13. Repeat reservation details to guest for confirmation.
  • 14. Thank guest and close call.

sops.title: Tula Sequence of Service — Overview & The Amrit Way
important_rules: Amrit is dedicated to redefining the dining experience — exceptional, personalized, Forbes Five-Star quality.
Consistency is everything — every guest, every table, every shift.
Never say 'No' to a guest — always offer an alternative.
Follow the 10-5 rule: eye contact at 10 feet, verbal greeting at 5 feet.
Never ask 'Do you have a reservation?' — it makes guests feel unwelcome.

sections.title: The Amrit Way
sections.body_html: The following 18 standards define the complete guest journey at Tula, from Host Greeting through Table Reset. Every team member is responsible for knowing and executing each standard at all times. These are not guidelines — they are the service.

sops.title: Tula Steps of Service — Beverage Order Delivery, Intro to the Menu & Specials
important_rules: Goal: To deliver beverages promptly, introduce the menu and specials, and take the appetizer order efficiently.

sections.title: Beverage Order Delivery, Intro to the Menu & Specials
sections.body_html:
• Timeliness: Deliver beverages within five minutes.
  • Communication: Report any delays immediately to a Manager.
  • Order Handling: The first drink on the ticket should be picked up first.
  • Cocktail Preparation: Garnish cocktails according to the recipe. Ensure all cocktails are visually appealing and consistent.
  • Delivery: Use a tray for all beverage deliveries. Handle glassware by the base or stem.
  • Serving Order: Serve position one at the table first. Serve the Host last, even if they are in the first position.
  • Service Technique: Use open-hand service. Serve beverages with your right hand to the right of the Guest whenever possible. If serving from the left, use your left hand.
  • Identification: State each beverage by name as you place it in front of the Guest to confirm it is the correct drink.
  • Napkins & Coasters: All beverages without stemware must be served with napkins or coasters. Replace them promptly if spills occur.
  • Menu Introduction: Describe menu sections, sauces, and side dishes. Assist Guests in navigating the menu to enhance their experience. Example: “The menu features bites and small plates on the left, and larger plates and sides on the right.”
  • Appetizer Order
  • Suggestions: Make at least two appetizer suggestions. Example: “Would you like to start with an appetizer, such as our (insert appetizer name)?”
  • Menu Descriptions: Indicate which plates are intended for sharing but allow Guests to order as they prefer.
  • Timing: If Guests need more time to decide, return within three minutes to take their order.

sops.title: Tula Steps of Service — Beverage Refills

sections.title: Beverage Refills
sections.body_html:
• Goal To ensure that Guests are offered and provided beverage refills in a timely manner.
  • Behaviors
  • Offering Refills:
  • Offer refills when the standard refill levels are reached:
  • Non-alcoholic drinks: Offer when 1/3 full.
  • Bottled water: Offer when 1/5 bottle remains.
  • Alcoholic beverages: Offer when 1/5 drink remains.
  • Service:
  • Ensure no empty glasses or cups are left on the table. Remove promptly using a tray.
  • Maintain a dry table, free of spillage, condensation, or wetness.

sops.title: Tula Steps of Service — Check Presentation & Payment Process
important_rules: Important Touchpoints:

sections.title: Check Presentation & Payment Process
sections.body_html:
• Goal To present the check and process payment correctly.
  • Behaviors
  • Presentation:
  • Present the check after the Guest has declined additional service. Verify items at a side station before presenting.
  • Use a clean check presenter with a pen and promotional materials. Ensure no stains or green/red stripes are present.
  • Process:
  • Indicate to the Host that you will pick up the check at their convenience.
  • Ask if they need valet service. Monitor the table and return within one minute for payment.
  • Payment Handling:
  • Process credit card payments using the chip reader. Alert MOD for any malfunctions.
  • Return change or receipts within two minutes. Use proper verbiage for gratuity if included. Mention any “Evergreen” promotions.
  • Scenarios:
  • Single Card: Process with POS terminal.
  • Multiple Cards: Process with POS terminal and distribute receipts.
  • Cash: Match the check total, deliver change and receipt promptly.
  • Cash and Card: Enter amount paid by card, then cash.
  • Gift Card: Seek MOD assistance if needed.
  • Save all receipts for potential re-openings in the POS system.
  • Continue providing service until the Guest leaves. Politely ask for a signature if needed. Notify the Manager if the Guest leaves without signing.

sops.title: Tula Steps of Service — Clearing and Removal of Courses (After Each Course)
important_rules: Important Touchpoints:

sections.title: Clearing and Removal of Courses (After Each Course)
sections.body_html:
• Goal To ensure the proper procedure for clearing and removing silverware and plateware after each course.
  • Behaviors
  • Assessment:
  • Determine if you can clear the table by yourself or if you need assistance. Obtain necessary help discreetly.
  • Approach:
  • Approach the Guest from their right side to remove items. Position yourself to minimize disruption.
  • Request to Clear:
  • Politely ask if the Guest is finished with their plate:
  • “May I clear that plate for you?”
  • “May I remove this plate out of the way for you?”
  • Clearing:
  • Clear only when everyone is finished or if a plate has been pushed forward.
  • If food remains on the plate and was satisfactory, ask if they would like it packed to go.
  • Clearing Technique:
  • Clear from the right with the right hand, placing soiled items in the left hand.
  • Clear the heaviest soiled plate first, keeping it in your hand and place the second plate on your forearm.
  • Handling Silverware:
  • Place all silverware on the plate, with knives parallel to your arm and forks and spoons perpendicular to prevent spillage.
  • Avoid over-stacking to prevent injury or spillage.
  • Table Maintenance:
  • Wipe tables with a damp, folded linen and crumb into a share plate.
  • Return soiled dishes to the dish station, separating items into proper containers:
  • Silverware: Remove by the handle, place in cleaning solution. Discard silverware properly.
  • Glassware: Remove by the stem/base without touching the rim. Place in appropriate racks.
  • Plateware: Scrape remaining food into a trashcan, then place in bus tub. Separate for serving and share plates.
  • Guest Interaction:
  • If a Guest leaves temporarily, replace linens with fresh ones.
  • Some dishes may require a finger bowl after clearing.
  • Crumbing:
  • Crumb the table between courses as needed, starting with position one and moving clockwise.
  • Use a folded linen napkin to lift and brush crumbs to the edge of the table, tapping them onto a B&B plate. Never brush crumbs to the floor.
  • If a Guest is unhappy with their food, apologize, and notify the Manager immediately.
  • If you drop an item on a Guest, apologize, clean the debris, and notify the Manager. Offer dry laundry service.
  • If a Guest wants to keep their plate, leave it and clear it when they are finished.

sops.title: Tula Steps of Service — Coffee & Tea Presentation, Nightcap Offer

sections.title: Coffee & Tea Presentation, Nightcap Offer
sections.body_html:
• Goal To handle coffee and tea orders and delivery, and offer nightcaps properly.
  • Behaviors
  • Tea Service:
  • Present the tea box and flavor offerings. Confirm tea preferences and ask about milk, cream, sugar, honey, and lemon.
  • Steep tea in the teapot and serve hot tea within five minutes or as requested.
  • Coffee Service:
  • Confirm regular or decaf. Ask when the Guest would like their coffee served.
  • Make and pick up coffee, starting with cappuccinos/lattes, then espressos.
  • Introduce the coffee by brand and blend, place handle at the four o’clock position on the saucer, with the spoon behind it. Place condiments in the middle of the table.
  • Serving:
  • Serve coffee and tea from the right with the right hand. Set any condiments in the middle of the table.
  • Offer refills and ask if the Guest needs anything else.
  • Nightcap Offer:
  • After desserts and drinks, offer a nightcap or espresso. Follow clearing and removal standards for any empty glasses.

sops.title: Tula Steps of Service — Food Delivery

sections.title: Food Delivery
sections.body_html:
• Goal Deliver food in a timely and professional manner.
  • Delivery Times
  • Appetizer: 6-12 minutes
  • Entrée: 12-18 minutes
  • Dessert: 8-12 minutes
  • Note: Inform Guests of longer cook times for certain items, like whole fish or well-done steak.
  • Delivery Procedures
  • Verify meal accuracy and presentation before serving.
  • Hand carry food items; use a tray only if carrying more than three dishes.
  • Serve an entire table together. If necessary, enlist a partner to help.
  • Serve position one first, moving clockwise, with the Host served last, even if they are in the first position.
  • Present and introduce each course clearly, stating any allergies. Announce each item as you place it.
  • Serve food from the left with the left hand.
  • Never auction food; use seat positions for delivery.
  • Describe the dish with the type of protein, cooking method, and sauce or seasoning.
  • Place non-shared food items with the main item facing the Guest in the six o’clock position.
  • For shared dishes, place them in the center of the table and place sharing silverware on the edge of the plates.
  • Ask if Guests need anything else before leaving the table.

sops.title: Tula Steps of Service — Food Order
important_rules: Goal: To take food orders promptly, assist with decision-making, and address dietary needs or allergy concerns.
Important Touchpoints:

sections.title: Food Order
sections.body_html:
• Order Timing: Offer to take the order within 10 minutes of Guest seating. Examples: “May I answer any questions about the menu?” or “Would you like to place an order?”
  • Dietary Restrictions: Inquire about dietary restrictions or allergies. Example: “Are there any food allergies we should be aware of?” Follow the Last Call Hospitality Allergy/Dietary Restrictions Procedure if there are allergies.
  • Recommendations: Offer two entree recommendations sincerely based on the Guest’s preferences.
  • Order Confirmation: Record the order using the Toast handheld, repeat it to the Guest, and confirm accuracy. Example: “The yellowtail snapper is an excellent choice. Thank you.”
  • Course Explanation: Explain how the meal will be coursed unless otherwise requested. Example: “I’ll bring the Chili Mussels and Grilled Artichoke first, followed by the Wild Mushroom Pizza, Lobster Ravioli, and Chicken Parmesan. Is that correct?”
  • Steak Orders: Confirm steak cooking descriptions. Example: “How would you like your eight-ounce filet cooked? Medium will be a warm pink center. Will that be okay?”
  • Seat Numbering: Ensure seat numbering is correct to avoid mistakes when delivering food.
  • Attention to Guest: Avoid spending excessive time looking at the handheld. Step away from the table if order entry takes longer than five seconds.
  • Course Timing: Appropriately time courses with the Guest(s).
  • Kids’ Orders: Mark kid’s meals in the POS with a note saying “For Kids” to ensure prompt preparation.
  • Cooking Temperatures:
  • Rare: Cool red center
  • Medium Rare: Warm red center
  • Medium: Warm pink center
  • Medium Well: Slight pink center
  • Well Done: Grey center, cooked through
  • Amrit Allergy/Dietary Restrictions Procedure
  • Detail Inquiry: Ask for specific details about any mentioned allergies.
  • Record Details: Note allergies and details to communicate effectively with the kitchen and service team.
  • Manager & Chef Communication: Inform the Manager and Chef to verify if adjustments can be made.
  • Confirm Adjustments: Ensure the dish can be modified according to the allergy.
  • Order Notation: Mark the ticket with “ALLERGY.”
  • Kitchen Verification: Double-check with the kitchen to confirm proper preparation.
  • Dish Marking: Ensure the dish is clearly marked.
  • Manager Delivery: Dishes with allergies must be delivered by a Manager.
  • Guest Notes: The Manager should note the allergy in the Guest’s OpenTable profile.

sops.title: Tula Steps of Service — Guest Satisfaction Check (After Any Course or Beverage

sections.title: Guest Satisfaction Check (After Any Course or Beverage

sops.title: Tula Steps of Service — Host Greeting
important_rules: Goal: To provide every guest with a warm and friendly welcome within one minute of entering the restaurant, setting the tone for a hospitable dining experience.
Important Touchpoints:

Quote from Danny Meyer: “Virtually nothing else is as important as how one is made to feel in any business transaction. Hospitality exists when you believe the other person is on your side. The conver

sections.title: Host Greeting
sections.body_html:
• Follow the 10-5 rule: make eye contact with a guest when they are 10 feet away and verbally welcome them when they are 5 feet away.
  • Always deliver a warm and genuine welcome to the guest upon arrival.
  • "Good afternoon/evening, welcome to Tula!"
  • "Are you joining us in the dining room or visiting the bar?"
  • Never ask, "Do you have a reservation?" as it may make guests feel unwelcome.
  • Once their desired destination is ascertained, follow these steps:
  • Bar: Escort the guest to the bar and introduce them to the bartender.
  • Dining Room: Ask for their telephone number and input it into OpenTable.
  • If their name appears in OpenTable and the table is ready, proceed to "Seating the Guest."
  • If their name does not appear in the reservation list, use the "Timeline" view to check availability. Inform the guest about table availability.
  • If a table is available, create an OpenTable profile for them and seat them.
  • If no table is available, add them to the "Waitlist" with an accurate wait time.
  • Offer to seat waiting guests at the bar for a drink.
  • Always provide guests with multiple dining and drinking options to make them feel comfortable and welcome.
  • Update notes in OpenTable appropriately, differentiating between "Visit Notes" and "Guestbook Notes."
  • Never say "No" to a guest request; always offer alternative solutions and ensure accurate information.
  • Acknowledge waiting guests with a smile and let them know you will be right with them.

sops.title: Tula Steps of Service — Presentation of Dessert and After Dinner Drinks Menus

sections.title: Presentation of Dessert and After Dinner Drinks Menus
sections.body_html:
• Goal To present dessert and after-dinner drinks menus properly.
  • Behaviors
  • Presentation:
  • Ensure menus are clean, tear-free, and debris-free.
  • Present menus starting with position one and move clockwise, presenting the Host last.
  • Recommendations:
  • Make knowledgeable recommendations and review specialty desserts.
  • Mention two offerings from the after-dinner drink menu that complement the dessert.
  • Complimentary Desserts:
  • Do not discuss complimentary desserts. Special offerings must be approved by a Manager.
  • Order Taking:
  • Take orders by position, moving clockwise, and take the Host’s order last.
  • Serving:
  • Ensure the table is cleared before serving.
  • Serve after-dinner drinks using a tray. Serve from the right with the right hand and set a dessert spoon to the right of the main plate.
  • Place share plates directly in front of the Guest, not in the center of the table.

sops.title: Tula Steps of Service — Proper Wine Service

sections.title: Proper Wine Service
sections.body_html:
• Goal Provide Guests with appropriate service based on their wine order.
  • Bottled Wine Service
  • Always carry a captain’s corkscrew.
  • Serve wine within five minutes of the order.
  • Prepare appropriate glassware, ensuring it is spot and odor-free.
  • Mark the table with correct glassware:
  • All-purpose glasses – for red, white, and rosé wines.
  • Champagne flute – for champagne and sparkling wines.
  • Remove unnecessary glassware.
  • Place glassware to the left of the water glass.
  • Seek Manager assistance if uncomfortable opening a bottle.
  • Opening a Bottle of Wine
  • Approach the table with the wine cradled in your palm on a serviette.
  • Present the bottle to the Host for approval before opening.
  • Remove foil using the corkscrew, cutting at the second lip evenly, and pocket the foil.
  • Wipe the lip and top of the bottle with a black linen if debris is present.
  • Insert the corkscrew into the center of the cork, turn clockwise, and pull the cork out quietly.
  • Present the cork to the Host on their right side.
  • Ensure the bottle lip is clean before serving.
  • If the cork breaks:
  • Re-insert the wine key worm into the cork and proceed with service.
  • Finish opening the bottle away from the table if necessary.
  • Strain wine through a cheesecloth or Regency Wrap into a decanter if needed.
  • Serving a Bottle of Wine
  • Hold the bottle with the label facing the Guest.
  • Pour approximately one ounce into the Host's glass for tasting and approval.
  • Host approves taste before serving others.
  • Serve wine clockwise, with the Host served last.
  • Pour two to three ounces per glass.
  • Twist the bottle slightly after each pour to avoid drips; use a linen napkin for drips.
  • Place extra red wine on the table above the Host's glass with the label facing Guests.
  • Store extra white wine and rosé in a wine bucket near the Host, draped with a linen napkin.
  • Remove the cork from the table if requested.
  • Refill glasses when they are 1/5 empty.
  • Provide a fresh glass for the Host if a second bottle of the same wine is ordered.
  • Follow the same procedure for a different wine bottle order.
  • Sparkling Wine Service
  • Prepare glassware as per the wine service procedure, using only flutes for champagne or sparkling wine.
  • Serve sparkling wine clockwise, starting with position one and ending with the Host.
  • Pour in a single stream and avoid overfilling glasses due to carbonation.
  • Opening a Bottle of Sparkling Wine
  • Verify the label and vintage before approaching the table.
  • Present the bottle to the Host for approval.
  • Remove foil and pocket it.
  • Hold the bottle with one hand, thumb over the cork.
  • Point the bottle away from Guests and staff.
  • Loosen the wire guard and cover the cork with a napkin.
  • Turn the bottle slowly to release the cork quietly.
  • Present the cork to the Host with the vineyard label visible.
  • Serving a Bottle of Sparkling Wine
  • Hold the bottle with the label facing the Guest.
  • Pour a small amount into the Host's glass for tasting and approval.
  • Serve clockwise, avoiding overfilling glasses.
  • Twist the bottle slightly after each pour to avoid drips; use a linen napkin for drips.
  • Store partial bottles in a wine bucket and remove empty bottles.
  • Drape the bucket with a linen napkin.

sops.title: Tula Steps of Service — Resetting the Table

sections.title: Resetting the Table
sections.body_html:
• Goal To reset the table properly after Guests leave.
  • Behaviors
  • Setup:
  • Deliver flatware, glassware, plateware, and linens to each table. Handle items appropriately:
  • Glassware: Handle by the base or stem, avoid touching the rim.
  • Silverware: Handle by the handle, avoid touching the tips or blades.
  • Plateware: Handle by the edge, avoid touching the bowl.
  • Verification:
  • Check for cleanliness and damage: no chips, clean plateware, and unbent flatware.
  • Organization:
  • Ensure the table is organized with all necessary items. Reset immediately after Guests leave.
  • Cleaning:
  • Wipe the table and seating, sweep or pick up food and trash from under the table.
  • Tableware Placement:
  • Place fork to the left of the plate, knife to the right, and napkins folded correctly on top of share plates.
  • Place water and wine glasses in their proper positions.
  • Final Check:
  • Ensure the table is symmetrical and aligned. Quietly reposition chairs and inspect for quality assurance.

sops.title: Tula Steps of Service — Seating the Guest & Menu Presentation
important_rules: Goal: To escort the guest to their table comfortably and professionally, and to provide them with the restaurant menu.
Important Touchpoints:

sections.title: Seating the Guest & Menu Presentation
sections.body_html:
• The manager or lead host directs the guest to the seater using the guest's name.
  • "Will you please escort the Garcia party to their table?"
  • If the party includes children or ADA guests, ensure the table is prepared accordingly.
  • "Your table is being prepared; we will seat you as soon as possible."
  • The lead host provides the seater with two copies of the guest chit from OpenTable.
  • The seater obtains the correct number of menus and ensures tables are preset and leveled.
  • If necessary, carry drinks on a tray when locating guests at the bar.
  • Ask guests if they would like to transfer their bar tab to their table.
  • "Would you prefer your check to be transferred to your table?"
  • Offer a children’s activity if applicable.
  • "Here is your menu and activity."
  • Escort guests to their table, maintaining a comfortable pace and engaging in polite conversation.
  • If the guest refuses the table, accommodate their request unless it is reserved or needed for a large party. Offer alternative options.
  • Offer chair assistance for all guests, particularly elderly ones.
  • Present menus to each guest, starting with seat number one and moving clockwise.
  • "Mrs. Garcia, this is our cocktail menu, a wine list, and lunch/dinner menu."
  • Wish the guest a good meal and mention the server's name.
  • "I hope you enjoy your time with us this evening… Your server, [server's name], will be with you momentarily."
  • Guests have the right of way in the restaurant. Always S.T.O.P. for our guests (Step aside, Talk, Open gestures, Proceed).
  • Notify the manager immediately if a guest seems unhappy.
  • Perform a "Table Status Update" for the lead host upon returning to the host stand.
  • Menus must be clean and free of stains or tears.

sops.title: Tula Steps of Service — Server Greeting & Beverage Service
important_rules: Goal: To provide every guest with a warm and friendly welcome within one minute of sitting and to offer a description of the restaurant concept along with beverage recommendations.
Important Touchpoints:

sections.title: Server Greeting & Beverage Service
sections.body_html:
• Obtain the OpenTable guest chit before approaching the table to address guests by name.
  • Greet the table within one minute.
  • "Good afternoon/evening Mr. Smith, welcome to [restaurant name]. I am [server's name], and it is my pleasure to welcome you this evening."
  • Stand in an open position without leaning on the table or crossing arms.
  • Mention the restaurant concept.
  • "[Restaurant name] focuses on a modern interpretation of Latin Fusion cuisine brought to you by our chef [chef's name]."
  • Welcome back repeat guests and inform them of any menu changes.
  • Offer beverage service and an overview of the menu.
  • "Our cocktail list is based on our Latian Fusion Concept. May I suggest the ...?"
  • If a guest asks about specialty cocktails, point out the cocktail program and suggest handcrafted cocktails based on preferences.
  • For wine requests, direct guests to the Wine by the Glass options and make pairing suggestions.
  • Take beverage orders by position, moving clockwise and taking the host's order last or Ladies first with the Host being last.
  • Ask for specific spirit preferences and mention brands to upsell without overselling.
  • Enter all beverage orders into the POS system.
  • Repeat the order back to the guest to ensure accuracy.
  • Ask questions to determine guest preferences, such as:
  • "What spirits do you prefer?"
  • "What is your favorite cocktail?"
  • "What do you like to drink at home?"
  • "Would you prefer something refreshing, bitter, sweet, or citrus-flavored?"
  • "Would you prefer something shaken or stirred?"
  • Ensure familiarity with the restaurant's wine, specialty cocktails, beer, and other liquor brands.

sops.title: Tula Steps of Service — Table Marking (Before Any Course)

sections.title: Table Marking (Before Any Course)
sections.body_html:
• Goal Ensure Guests have the proper silverware and plateware for each course of their meal.
  • Behaviors
  • Serve all silverware from a lined show plate.
  • Serve silverware to Guests clockwise by position number, with the Host served last, even if they are in the first position.
  • Preset silverware one course at a time, before the course is served.
  • Place silverware in the following order:
  • Knives from the right side with the right hand, facing the plate.
  • Spoons from the right side with the right hand, to the right of the knives.
  • Forks on the left side with the left hand.
  • Keep tables neat and tidy, ensuring appropriate silverware and condiments are present before each course.
  • Serve flatware with the food.
  • Replace share plates and silverware as needed, typically after every two courses.
  • Determine and set appropriate silverware for each course based on the dishes to be served (e.g., steak knife for steak).
  • Important Touchpoints
  • Use a marking tray for all flatware, with handles visible. Touch flatware only by handles and plateware only by the rim or edges.
  • Do not preset share plates if Guests are not sharing food items.

sops.title: Tula Steps of Service — The Fond Farewell

sections.title: The Fond Farewell
sections.body_html:
• Goal To provide a warm and appreciative farewell to Guests.
  • Behaviors
  • Acknowledgment:
  • Thank the Guest by name and acknowledge their visit.
  • “It was a pleasure having you with us today. Enjoy the rest of your evening.”
  • “Mrs. Garcia, thank you for visiting us! We hope to see you again soon!”
  • Assistance:
  • Assist with future reservations or requests.
  • Partially escort Guests to the exit and open doors if possible.
  • Farewell:
  • Bid farewell to all departing Guests. Use their name when known and invite them back warmly.
  • Notes:
  • Relay any relevant notes from the dining experience to the Lead Host or MOD for updating the Guest’s OpenTable profile.
  • Check for Belongings:
  • Ensure the Guest does not leave anything behind.

sops.title: Tula Steps of Service — Water Service
important_rules: Goal: To offer guests water based on their preference.

sections.title: Water Service
sections.body_html:
• Offer bottled water by name.
  • "Would you care for bottled water? We have Saratoga Still or Sparkling. Or would you prefer ice water?" Never say Tap water
  • Approach the table with water, placing a beverage napkin and glasses appropriately.
  • Carry bottles by the base, not the neck, with the label facing the table.
  • Pour water for each guest from the right side, filling the glass ¾ full without spills.
  • Use a folded linen napkin as a splash guard and tilt the bottle away from the table.
  • Serve clockwise, with the host last.
  • Refill Standards:
  • Refill regular water glasses when they are 1/3 empty.
  • Refill bottled water glasses when they are 1/5 empty.
  • For bottled water, ask guests if they would like an additional bottle when it is 1/5 empty.

sops.title: Tula Steps of Service — Wine Selection Offer

sections.title: Wine Selection Offer
sections.body_html:
• Goal Assist the Guest in selecting a wine that is suitable for their dining experience.
  • Behaviors
  • Offer wine using suitable questions:
  • “May I suggest a wine to pair with your meal?”
  • “May I be of assistance with the wine list?”
  • “Are you looking for a white or a red wine this evening?”
  • “What type of wine do you normally enjoy?”
  • If the Guest requests a wine pairing, offer two options at different price points. Recommend food wines that complement the meal without overwhelming the palate.
  • Assess the Guest's willingness to try new wines. If trust is established, suggest unique wines from different regions.
  • Important: Do not oversell to your Guests.
  • For parties of four or more, recommend two bottles to cater to varied preferences.
  • Ensure thorough wine knowledge, including categories (light-bodied, medium-bodied, full-bodied) and pairings with land and sea dishes.
  • Confirm the wine order with the Guest and enter it into the POS system.
  • If uncomfortable, excuse yourself politely and involve a Manager immediately.

sops.title: End-of-Shift Release SOP
important_rules: Team members must check with MOD before completing shift or leaving building.
MOD must verify responsibilities complete and business levels allow release.
Unauthorized leaving is subject to discipline up to termination.

sections.title: Procedure
sections.body_html:
• 1. When shift end approaches, check with MOD for release.
  • 2. MOD verifies all shift responsibilities are fulfilled.
  • 3. MOD confirms business levels allow departure.
  • 4. Check with MOD again before leaving building.
  • 5. Leave only after explicit authorization.

sops.title: Shift Break Approval SOP
important_rules: All breaks require MOD approval before taken.
Unauthorized breaks are disciplinary violations.

sections.title: Procedure
sections.body_html:
• 1. Before taking a break, check with MOD.
  • 2. MOD confirms business levels allow break.
  • 3. MOD approves break timing to keep operations organized.
  • 4. Take break only after approval.

sops.title: Standards of Professional Conduct and Appearance SOP
important_rules: Professional attire and appearance required in guest-facing areas.
Service team shirts must be tucked and sleeves rolled down.
Female employees must keep hair secured/neat.
Cell phones must not be visible or used in front-of-house.
Personal reading materials during shift are not permitted.
On-duty socializing with team members is not allowed.

sections.title: Procedure
sections.body_html:
• 1. Maintain professional appearance in all guest-facing areas.
  • 2. Keep shirt tucked in and sleeves rolled down (service teams).
  • 3. Keep hair secured in ponytail or professional neat style (female employees).
  • 4. Keep cell phones out of sight and do not use in front-of-house.
  • 5. Do not read personal books/materials during shift.
  • 6. Do not socialize on duty; focus on responsibilities and guest engagement.

sops.title: Beach & Pool Attendant Closing Duties Checklist
important_rules: Confirm guests are out of area before shutdown.
Return walkie-talkie to F&B office.
Notify supervisor when duties are complete.

steps.text: 1. Confirm all guests have left beach and pool areas.
2. Collect/store equipment (chairs, umbrellas, cabanas, tables).
3. Gather service items (towels, trays, menus, coolers, amenities).
4. Inspect equipment and report damage/missing items.
5. Remove trash/debris and wipe tables/surfaces.
6. Return all towels to laundry area.
7. Ensure area is clean, organized, and ready for next day.
8. Return walkie-talkie to F&B office.
9. Notify supervisor once all closing duties are completed.
10. Check with manager for additional tasks before leaving.
11. Print checklists as needed.

sops.title: Beach & Pool Attendant Guest Service Duties (Ongoing)
important_rules: Guest safety and comfort monitoring is continuous during shift.

sections.title: Procedure
sections.body_html:
• 1. Greet and assist guests with seating preferences.
  • 2. Offer towels, water, and amenities promptly.
  • 3. Monitor guest safety and comfort throughout day.
  • 4. Maintain cleanliness of pool deck and beach pathways.
  • 5. Collect used towels and replace with fresh towels as needed.
  • 6. Check cabanas regularly and restock amenities.
  • 7. Communicate guest requests to appropriate departments.

sops.title: Beach & Pool Attendant Opening Duties Checklist
important_rules: Collect walkie-talkie from F&B office and set to Channel 5.
Area must be clean and equipment safe before guest use.

steps.text: 1. Set up chairs, umbrellas, cabanas, and tables.
2. Stock towels, water, and guest amenities.
3. Inspect all equipment for safety and functionality.
4. Ensure beach/pool area is clean and free of debris.
5. Collect walkie-talkie and set to Channel 5.

sops.title: Beach Attendant Closing Procedures SOP
important_rules: All equipment must be collected, cleaned, and safely stored.
Return walkie-talkie to F&B office.
Report lost & found and damaged/missing items.

sections.title: Procedure
sections.body_html:
• 1. Gather lounge chairs, umbrellas, cushions, mats, side tables.
  • 2. Close umbrellas carefully and remove from sand.
  • 3. Pack sand around poles as needed for safe storage.
  • 4. Clean and wipe down furniture/equipment.
  • 5. Park ATV in designated area and check fuel/basic safety.
  • 6. Turn off and lock ATV if applicable.
  • 7. Return walkie-talkie to F&B office.
  • 8. Clean beach area of trash/debris/personal items.
  • 9. Report lost & found items to supervisor.
  • 10. Record damaged/missing chairs in daily log.
  • 11. Report guest complaints regarding chairs immediately.

sops.title: Beach Attendant Daily Closing Checklist
important_rules: Confirm all guests have left before closing activities.
ATV must be cleaned, parked, and secured.
Notify supervisor after completion.

steps.text: 1. Confirm all guests have left beach area.
2. Collect/store equipment (chairs, umbrellas, tables, cabana items).
3. Gather/return service items (towels, trays, menus, coolers).
4. Inspect equipment and report damage/missing items.
5. Remove trash/debris; wipe tables/surfaces.
6. Clean and park ATV in designated storage; secure it.
7. Return walkie-talkie to F&B office.
8. Ensure beach area is clean, organized, and ready for next day.
9. Notify supervisor closing duties are complete.

sops.title: Beach Attendant Daily Opening/Setup Checklist
important_rules: Set walkie-talkie to Channel 5.
Inspect ATV safety before use and operate safely.

steps.text: 1. Greet guests warmly upon arrival.
2. Set up chairs, umbrellas, cabanas, tables.
3. Stock towels, water, and amenities.
4. Inspect equipment for safety/functionality.
5. Ensure beach area is clean and debris-free.
6. Collect walkie-talkie from F&B office and set Channel 5.
7. Inspect ATV fuel/tire pressure/safety before use.
8. Use ATV safely to transport equipment and supplies.

sops.title: Beach Attendant Opening Procedures SOP
important_rules: Walkie-talkie must be set to Channel 5.
Inspect equipment and ATV before operations.
Follow layout and safety spacing for setup.

sections.title: Procedure
sections.body_html:
• 1. Retrieve walkie-talkie from F&B office and set Channel 5.
  • 2. Collect cleaning tools, chairs, umbrellas, cushions, towels, mats, side tables.
  • 3. Arrange lounge chairs per resort layout with proper spacing.
  • 4. Inspect chairs for damage and remove/repair defective units.
  • 5. Select stable umbrella positions away from high tide/crowds.
  • 6. Dig hole as needed, insert umbrella, secure with sand/anchor/sandbags.
  • 7. Open umbrellas and tilt into wind (10–15°).
  • 8. Place towels, cushions, mats, and side tables neatly.
  • 9. Inspect ATV (fuel, brakes, tires, lights).
  • 10. Use ATV only on designated paths for transport.
  • 11. Complete final walkthrough confirming setup and working communications.

sops.title: Beach Attendant Responsibilities SOP
important_rules: Report damage/missing equipment/incidents immediately.
Operate ATVs safely and on designated paths.
Keep walkie-talkie communication active.
Follow all resort safety/hygiene protocols.

sections.title: Procedure
sections.body_html:
• 1. Set up and maintain lounge chairs, umbrellas, and beach equipment.
  • 2. Operate/store ATV safely.
  • 3. Use walkie talkie to communicate with team.
  • 4. Keep beach area clean, organized, and safe.
  • 5. Greet guests warmly and assist with seating/cabana setup.
  • 6. Provide beach amenity/activity/resort information.
  • 7. Anticipate guest needs and deliver prompt courteous service.
  • 8. Stock towels, water, and amenities at service points.
  • 9. Remove used towels, trash, and debris throughout day.
  • 10. Inspect equipment condition and safety.
  • 11. Rake algae from shoreline as needed.
  • 12. Support food and beverage service when applicable.
  • 13. Coordinate with housekeeping, F&B, and front office.
  • 14. Report guest requests/incidents/feedback promptly.
  • 15. Use safe lifting techniques and secure umbrellas/chairs.
  • 16. Do not leave umbrellas unattended in high winds.
  • 17. Log incidents, damages, and supply shortages before shift end.

sops.title: Beach Weather Call SOP
important_rules: Make weather call for high wind, lightning proximity, heavy rain/storm forecast, or dangerous high tide.
Use Channel 5 for team communication.
Prioritize guest/staff safety over equipment.
Do not operate ATVs during hazardous weather.
Reopen only with supervisor confirmation.

sections.title: Procedure
sections.body_html:
• 1. Check weather reports at shift start.
  • 2. Continuously monitor sky and wind conditions.
  • 3. Watch for lightning, gusts, and approaching storms.
  • 4. Trigger weather call when thresholds or severe conditions are met.
  • 5. Notify beach supervisor immediately.
  • 6. Communicate team actions via walkie-talkie Channel 5.
  • 7. Inform guests politely and assist relocation as needed.
  • 8. Close/remove umbrellas and secure loose items.
  • 9. Stack/store lounge chairs.
  • 10. Secure mats, cushions, towels, side tables; park ATVs safely.
  • 11. Resume operations only after supervisor confirms safe conditions.
  • 12. Re-check equipment for damage before reopening.

sops.title: Pool Attendant SOP
important_rules: Maintain confidentiality of guest/resident information and lists.
Do not disclose names/room numbers/unit details to unauthorized persons.
Do not share/remove guest or resident lists from designated areas.

sections.title: Procedure
sections.body_html:
• 1. Arrive 30 minutes before opening.
  • 2. Collect resident and in-house guest lists from Front Desk/Manager.
  • 3. Inspect pool deck: remove debris, wipe/arrange furniture, open umbrellas, place fresh towels.
  • 4. Confirm towels are stocked/count opening inventory.
  • 5. Ensure amenities (sunscreen/water/trash) are clean and stocked.
  • 6. For hotel guests: request last name and room number, verify list, record guest name/room/towel count.
  • 7. For residents: verify resident list and record resident name or unit and towel count.
  • 8. For PT guests: verify against PT list and record details.
  • 9. Monitor towel stock and request replenishment before low.
  • 10. Walk pool deck every 30 minutes to remove used towels and reset area.
  • 11. Provide attentive, discreet, friendly service throughout shift.
  • 12. At end of day collect used towels and deliver to laundry.
  • 13. Record closing towel count and reconcile with logs.
  • 14. Clean/sanitize pool furniture and close umbrellas/secure equipment.
  • 15. Report maintenance/safety issues to manager.
  • 16. Submit towel log to Supervisor/Front Desk before leaving.
  • 17. Complete daily log without exception and report disputes/unusual activity immediately.

sops.title: Loss Prevention SOP
important_rules: All found/recovered items must go directly to Security immediately.
No exceptions; no employee storage/holding permitted.
Employees may not return items directly to guests.
Nothing leaves property unless released by Security.
Violations are zero-tolerance and subject to termination.

sections.title: Procedure
sections.body_html:
• 1. If an item is found/recovered/left behind, stop immediately and take it directly to Security.
  • 2. Do not hold or store item anywhere (lockers, offices, stations, kitchens, bars, bags).
  • 3. Do not give item to another employee to hold.
  • 4. Do not remove any item from property without Security release.
  • 5. Deliver high-value/sensitive items (cash, wallets, phones, jewelry, cards, keys, electronics) directly and immediately to Security.
  • 6. Refer all guest property inquiries to Security only.
  • 7. Do not search/store/retrieve items on behalf of guests.
  • 8. Ensure Security documents item receipt.
  • 9. Acknowledge that delays or non-compliance are policy violations subject to discipline.

sops.title: Safety Walk Inspections SOP
important_rules: Urgent deficiencies must be marked urgent in ALICE.
Escalate urgent hazards immediately.
Follow daily/weekly/monthly inspection cadence.

sections.title: Procedure
sections.body_html:
• 1. Prepare checklist, PPE, flashlight, camera; review prior reports and open corrective items.
  • 2. Conduct systematic inspection of designated area.
  • 3. Check housekeeping/cleanliness hazards.
  • 4. Check fire safety systems and exits.
  • 5. Check electrical conditions.
  • 6. Check mechanical/equipment conditions.
  • 7. Check pools/hydrothermal safety items.
  • 8. Check general safety (handrails, trip hazards, flooring, signage).
  • 9. Check security/access (locked doors, cameras, emergency exits).
  • 10. Document findings in ALICE Safety Walk Checklist.
  • 11. Photograph deficiencies and attach to ALICE tickets.
  • 12. Mark completion comments digitally in ALICE.
  • 13. Submit checklist to Engineering Director.
  • 14. Follow up and ensure corrective actions close within timelines.
  • 15. Track repeat issues for management review.

sops.title: Walkie Talkie SOP
important_rules: Team members must sign out radio and charger at shift start and sign in at return.
Default channel is Line 5 unless MOD authorizes change.
No personal conversations or unauthorized sharing.
Urgent situations must be called as Priority/Emergency.
Lost/damaged radios must be reported immediately.

sections.title: Procedure
sections.body_html:
• 1. At shift start, check in with manager and sign out radio + charger.
  • 2. Manager records team member name and device number.
  • 3. Inspect radio for charge and functionality.
  • 4. Turn on device and select assigned channel (default Line 5).
  • 5. Use brief professional communication and proper etiquette (identify self/location, wait 1–2 sec after press-to-talk, acknowledge with Copy/Received/On my way).
  • 6. Do not share with unauthorized personnel.
  • 7. For emergencies, transmit with Priority/Emergency and clear location/issue.
  • 8. At shift end return radio to manager in good condition.
  • 9. Record return time and sign device back in.
  • 10. Place radio on charger in designated location.
  • 11. Report malfunctions/damage/loss immediately.

sops.title: Spa Concierge — Attendance & Disciplinary Policy
important_rules: Attendance, punctuality, and professional conduct are expectations at Amrit Wellness & Spa — not aspirations. The following disciplinary framework applies to all Spa Concierge team members.

sections.title: Attendance Standards
sections.body_html:
• Arrive on time in full uniform and present at the assigned post at the scheduled shift start.
  • Late arrival must be communicated to management as early as possible — before the shift start time.
  • Unexcused absences or repeated tardiness will trigger the disciplinary process.

    • Progressive Discipline Process

    1. Verbal Warning: Documented conversation with management. Specific behavior, standard, and expectation communicated clearly.

    2. Written Warning: Formal written documentation of the violation, prior verbal warning, and corrective expectations. Signed by team member and manager.

    3. Final Warning: A final documented warning with a clear statement that continued violations will result in termination.

    4. Termination: Employment ended based on continued or severe violation of Amrit Wellness & Spa standards.

    Serious violations — including theft, guest misconduct, or gross insubordination — may bypass earlier steps and result in immediate termination, at management's discretion.

    Amrit Wellness & Spa | Amrit Ocean Resort & Residences | Singer Island, Florida | Version 2.0 — March 2026

sops.title: Spa Concierge — Attendance, Punctuality & Break Policy
important_rules: Reliability and punctuality are foundational to delivering consistent five-star service. The following standards apply to all Spa Concierge team members.
Arrive a minimum of 10 minutes before the scheduled shift start — in full uniform, ready to relieve the previous team member.
If late arrival is anticipated for any reason, notify management immediately — before the shift start time.
All breaks must be coordinated with and approved by the supervisor on duty.
The front desk must never be left unattended — coverage must be confirmed before any team member steps away.
Break coverage is assigned by the supervisor; team members do not self-assign coverage.
Repeated tardiness will be subject to the disciplinary action process outlined in Section 19.
Unplanned absences must be reported to management as early as possible — no less than two hours before shift start.

important_rules: Escorting a guest is a standard of luxury service. It eliminates confusion, demonstrates attentiveness, and elevates the experience from a transaction to a guided journey. Whenever staffing levels all

sops.title: Spa Concierge — Guest Arrival & Greeting Sequence
important_rules: Every guest arrival is a defining moment for the Amrit experience. The greeting sequence is deliberate, warm, and sequenced to create an immediate sense of being cared for. Follow every step, for ever

sections.title: Step 1 — Immediate Greeting (within 5 seconds of arrival)
sections.body_html:
Make eye contact and acknowledge the guest the moment they enter the spa lobby. Do not wait until they reach the desk. Begin moving toward them if they appear uncertain about where to go.

“Good morning / afternoon, Mr./Ms. [Last Name]. Welcome to Amrit Wellness & Spa. It is a pleasure to have you with us today. How may I assist you?”

[ IMAGE PLACEHOLDER — Concierge greeting guest at desk — upright posture, warm eye contact, both hands visible on desk ]

Step 2 — Determine Visit Type

Gracefully determine whether the guest has a reservation or is a walk-in. The tone must always be welcoming — never interrogating.

Walk-in (no reservation):

“Of course, allow me to walk you through today's availability and our wellness experiences.”

With reservation — confirmation found:

“Wonderful, Mr./Ms. [Last Name]. We have your [service] scheduled for [time]. Allow me to begin your check-in.”

With reservation — not found in system:

“I want to make sure we take excellent care of you. Allow me a moment to look into this.”

Note: Never make a guest feel unwelcome if a reservation cannot be immediately located. Investigate thoroughly and discreetly before escalating.

Step 3 — Welcome Ritual (Cold Towel)

The cold towel presentation is a sensory signal that the wellness experience has begun. Present with both hands on a tray — never handed directly from one hand.

“Please allow me to offer you a refreshing cold towel to begin your experience.”

[ IMAGE PLACEHOLDER — Cold towel presentation on tray — both hands extended, eye contact, slight incline forward ]

Follow with a welcome beverage offer if applicable (water, infused water, herbal tea). Note any dietary preferences or restrictions.

Step 4 — Guest Orientation

Guide the guest into the locker and facility orientation. Use specific directional language — never vague gestures.

“I will guide you to our locker area, where you will begin your wellness journey and enjoy the Ayush facilities.”

Step 5 — Set Expectations

Before the guest moves toward the locker level, set clear expectations for the remainder of their visit so they feel guided and at ease.

“Please make your way to the [2nd / 4th floor] waiting area at your scheduled time. Your therapist will personally welcome you. Please take this time to relax and enjoy the space.”

  • Answer all calls within three rings.

sections.title: 2.2 Conduct Standards
sections.body_html:
The Spa Concierge represents Amrit's commitment to Forbes Five-Star service. Conduct standards are non-negotiable in all guest-facing situations.

  • Always stand while assisting guests — never seated in guest view.
  • Personal phones are completely out of sight during working hours — no exceptions.

  • Calm, confident, and attentive at all times — regardless of pace or pressure.
  • No personal conversations with colleagues in guest-visible areas.
  • Speak softly and professionally in all guest areas.
  • Never eat, drink (non-water), or chew gum at the front desk.
  • Never point — always escort or provide clear directional guidance.

sops.title: Spa Concierge — Service Recovery Standards
important_rules: Service recovery is a defining moment of a luxury brand. How a team member responds to a delay, complaint, or service failure determines whether a guest leaves feeling let down or feeling genuinely ca

sections.title: Handling Delays
sections.body_html:
“Thank you for your patience, Mr./Ms. [Last Name]. We will have everything ready for you shortly.”

“I sincerely apologize for the delay. We are preparing your experience to the highest standard.”

Complaint Handling — 4-Step Recovery Model

1. Acknowledge

“Thank you for sharing this with me.”

Receive the complaint without interruption. Acknowledgment alone can de-escalate most situations.

2. Empathize

“I completely understand, and I am genuinely sorry to hear this.”

Validate the guest's experience. Never argue, justify, or deflect.

3. Take Ownership

“Please allow me to take care of this for you personally.”

Step into the role of problem solver. The guest should feel they have a champion.

4. Resolve

“I will ensure this is handled immediately and to your full satisfaction.”

Provide a clear, tangible next step. Escalate to supervisor if resolution is beyond your authority.

Critical rule: Never deflect. Never argue. Escalate to supervisor when a resolution is beyond your authority — and inform the guest that you are doing so, so they feel the matter is being elevated, not dismissed.

sops.title: Spa Concierge — Turtle Season Protocol (March 1 – October 31)
important_rules: Singer Island, Florida is an active sea turtle nesting corridor. Light pollution from resort buildings can disorient nesting sea turtles and hatchlings, causing them to move away from the ocean. Amrit
All blinds on the 3rd floor must be fully closed after dark for the entire Turtle Season period (March 1 through October 31).
Attendants are responsible for confirming blinds are fully closed on the 1st, 2nd, and 4th floors.
Any lights visible from the beach-facing side must be addressed immediately.
If a guest opens blinds in a restricted area, politely explain the protocol and close them again.
Any observed sea turtle nesting activity must be reported to management immediately — do not approach or disturb nesting turtles.
